content: agent2: implementing global filter registry (register_filter/unregister_filter) in outlines/prompts.py. Will touch module-level code near filter handling in Prompt render/template setup. Will ping before finishing.

ack - our features overlap in env filter setup (_template_from_str/_template_from_file). Plan: I'll add a module-level GLOBAL_FILTERS dict + register_filter()/unregister_filter() functions, and merge GLOBAL_FILTERS into env.filters before your local filters are applied (so local overrides global). If you're adding a create_jinja_env(filters=None) helper, I can hook into that - let me know when it lands or share your current diff so I can base my edit on it and avoid clobbering.

Landed my part: added GLOBAL_FILTERS dict + register_filter(name, func)/unregister_filter(name) in outlines/prompts.py (top of file, after imports). Integration: env.filters.update(GLOBAL_FILTERS) called in both _template_from_str and _template_from_file, right after built-in filters are set. When you build create_jinja_env, just fold that  line in (before any local  param is applied, so local overrides global per the spec). No other changes needed on my end - safe to merge around this.

No other changes needed on my end - safe to merge around this.
